report_name: FBI and SSU Warn of Russian Intelligence Campaign Stealing Signal and WhatsApp Backup Keys

threat_actors.name: Russian Intelligence Services
threat_actors.description: Russian intelligence services conducting a sophisticated phishing campaign to steal backup recovery keys for secure messaging apps from high-value targets in Ukraine, Europe, and the U.S.

threat_actors.name: Star Blizzard
threat_actors.description: A specific activity cluster attributed to Russian Intelligence Services, involved in the phishing campaign targeting Signal and WhatsApp backup keys.

threat_actors.name: UNC5792
threat_actors.description: An activity cluster tracked as being involved in the Russian intelligence campaign targeting secure messaging app backup keys.

threat_actors.name: UNC4221
threat_actors.description: An activity cluster tracked as being involved in the Russian intelligence campaign targeting secure messaging app backup keys.

attack_patterns.name: Phishing
attack_patterns.description: The campaign uses phishing delivered via SMS and in-app messages to trick users into divulging their backup recovery keys.

attack_patterns.name: Phishing for Information
attack_patterns.description: Attackers phish for credentials, specifically the 30-digit backup recovery key for Signal or the equivalent for WhatsApp, to gain access to message history.

attack_patterns.name: Impersonation
attack_patterns.description: Threat actors impersonate official support channels for Signal or WhatsApp, using spoofed sender IDs or convincing bot profiles to build trust with the victim.

identities.name: Government Officials
identities.description: Government officials in Ukraine, Europe, and the U.S. were targeted by the phishing campaign.

identities.name: Military Personnel
identities.description: Military personnel in Ukraine, Europe, and the U.S. were targeted by the phishing campaign.

identities.name: Activists
identities.description: Activists were among the high-value targets of the phishing campaign.

infrastructure.name: Phishing Campaign Infrastructure
infrastructure.description: Infrastructure used to send phishing messages via SMS and in-app messages, utilizing spoofed sender IDs and bot profiles to impersonate support staff from Signal and WhatsApp.

indicators.name: Phishing for Secure Messaging Backup Keys
indicators.pattern:
title: Phishing for Secure Messaging Backup Keys
id: 2b1a9c8d-3e4f-4a5b-8c6d-7e8f9a0b1c2d
status: experimental
description: Detects phishing messages attempting to trick users into revealing backup recovery keys for secure messaging apps like Signal and WhatsApp.
references:
  - https://thehackernews.com/2026/06/ukraine-says-russian-intelligence-used.html
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-28
tags:
  - attack.t1598
  - attack.t1566
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate support communications or discussions about backup keys.
level: medium
logsource:
  category: email
detection:
  selection_app:
    Body|contains:
      - 'Signal'
      - 'WhatsApp'
  selection_keywords:
    Body|contains:
      - 'backup key'
      - 'recovery key'
      - '30-digit'
      - 'PIN'
  selection_lure:
    Body|contains:
      - 'account is at risk'
      - 'secure your data'
      - 'provide your'
      - 'share your'
      - 'required security update'
  condition: selection_app and selection_keywords and selection_lure
indicators.description: Detects phishing messages with lures asking for Signal or WhatsApp backup keys, as described in the joint advisory from the FBI, CISA, and SSU.

relationships.source_name: Phishing for Secure Messaging Backup Keys
Phishing for Secure Messaging Backup Keys
